riskFactors: No public smart-contract audit report was found for BUIDL contracts in the approved source set.
EVM deployments use proxy-based upgradeability and admin-controlled components, including owner-controlled target updates on the original Ethereum BUIDL proxy.

Token transfers are permissioned/restricted through whitelist and compliance controls, reducing secondary-market liquidity and increasing operational dependency on Securitize/Transfer Agent infrastructure.
No public proof-of-reserves URL, attestation cadence, full reserve breakdown, collateralization ratio, or latest audit report URL was found in approved sources.
Reserve custodian and auditor are supported mainly by RWA.xyz and contextual media rather than a public primary fund document.
Secondary market liquidity is thin: DeFiLlama reports 24h volume of 0 USD and on-chain liquidity of 320,081 USD on Raydium AMM at the recorded snapshot.
Ethereum original BUIDL holder count is 109, indicating limited on-chain holder distribution for that contract and potential concentration risk.
BUIDL and BUIDL-I are separate token rows/contracts; incorrect aggregation can overstate supply, holders, liquidity, or chain coverage.
mitigants: Issuer identity and offering framework are anchored by SEC Form D, including Rule 506(c) and Investment Company Act Section 3(c)(7).
Issuer is associated with BlackRock, a large regulated asset manager, and product access is operated through Securitize.
Underlying reserve asset classes are cash, U.S. Treasury bills, and repurchase agreements, which are short-duration cash-equivalent and sovereign-linked instruments.
RWA.xyz reports Bank of New York Mellon as custodian and PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, although primary confirmation is still needed.
BUIDL has daily primary-market redemption through Securitize / Transfer Agent workflow, with 0% redemption fee and USD redemption processing through the fund according to the liquidity layer.

Multichain deployment across Ethereum, Solana, Avalanche, Aptos, BNB Chain, Optimism, Arbitrum, and Polygon reduces single-chain availability dependency, although operational controls remain permissioned.
Original Ethereum BUIDL and BUIDL-I contracts are verified on Etherscan, and several EVM deployments have readable explorer pages.
redFlags: No BlackRock-hosted public BUIDL product page, prospectus, private placement memorandum, or full fund documentation was found in the current source-discovery set.
No public proof-of-reserves or detailed holdings breakdown was found.
No public smart-contract audit report was found.
Transfer restrictions, whitelist controls, and proxy/admin controls require deeper implementation-level review before final production smart-contract scoring.
Public secondary liquidity is very limited despite large AUM/TVL figures.
Custodian and auditor fields need primary-source confirmation before institutional-grade finalization.
analystNotes: Scores are evidence-weighted quality scores where higher means lower observed risk / stronger mitigants. BUIDL receives strong issuer and legal scores due to SEC Form D, BlackRock association, Securitize infrastructure, and Treasury/cash-equivalent reserve profile. Scores are capped by missing primary fund documentation, lack of public proof-of-reserves or reserve breakdown, no public smart-contract audit report, proxy/admin controls, whitelist-based transfer restrictions, thin secondary liquidity, and low Ethereum original holder count. Do not treat TVL/AUM as liquidity; market size is large but secondary tradability is limited. Final production scoring should be revisited after primary fund documents, audit reports, attestation details, and implementation-level contract permissions are verified.
